Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: expert LRU cache, prefix caching, pinned H2D staging, batching engine

- Expert LRU cache: GPU pool for MoE host-resident experts with O(1) lookup,
  avoids repeated H2D copies (59.6% hit rate on Nemotron-30B)
- Content-addressed prefix caching: hash-chained KV block reuse for shared
  prefixes, blocks retained on free_sequence() with LRU eviction
- Pinned staging buffer: double-buffered cudaHostAlloc for true async DMA on
  WSL2 where mmap can't be pinned, cached VRAM state eliminates per-tensor
  cudaMemGetInfo calls
- Batching engine: background worker thread with ServerRequest token queue,
  concurrent request support via mutex+condvar
- Server: embeddings endpoint, think-budget param, prefix-caching flag
- Tests: 7 new content-addressed prefix caching tests

commit.message:
fix: resolve ORC ValidationFailedException + wire shared KB knowledge source

Root cause: MediaPlanResearchInitiation topic stored in DialogComponent format
(botContent.yml wrapper) with wrong auto_agent_GYB07 schema prefix.
Fix: rewrote as AdaptiveDialog with new_bot_433ffa224b1af1118342000d3a3165ab prefix.

Key findings:
- BeginDialog to InvokeConnectedAgentTaskAction causes runtime SystemError in v80
- MediaPlanResearchInitiation topic removed (intercepts generative responses)
- shared_kb (MCMAP/v8.0/shared/kb) added back to ORC — ID: 5d5840da
- Broad MCMAP/v8.0 KB path surfaces non-KB content (bad responses)
- ORC/kb specific path causes SystemError (SharePoint not yet indexed)
- Judge model: upgraded haiku → sonnet-4-6 for reliable KG scoring
- parallel_scenarios: 4 → 2 to reduce PA rate limit aborts
- FL-085 KBSearchInjection deployed: 54a63c96
- FL-086 OverlayContextLoader deployed: 4b32038b

L1-001 with shared_kb + sonnet judge: code_scorer_pass_rate=0.79, KG=20

Players must bring their own API keys for all 6 providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sync deadlock — only skip auth errors, expose internal error in health check

Root cause of accounts stuck since March 7:
- Any PlaidApiException (including transient ones) set last_error_code on the item
- sync-all then permanently skipped ALL items with ANY last_error_code
- The error is only cleared on success, but success never happens because the
  item is always skipped → permanent deadlock

Fix in sync-all: only skip items whose error_code is a genuine auth failure
(ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED, INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN, etc.) that requires user action.
Transient errors, our own sync errors, and past errors are retried normally.

Also:
- plaid_item_status: include internal_error_code/message/at fields so health
  check can distinguish Plaid auth errors from our own sync errors
- Health check UI: show 🟡 + amber "sync error" badge when internal_error_code
  is set, with explanatory message to distinguish from reconnect-required state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add onboarding protection to prevent account ID mismatches

CRITICAL FIX: Prevents data loss from account ID mismatches

Changes:
- OnboardingSimplified now checks Firestore for existing trades before showing UI
- If trades exist, auto-creates missing accounts and restores data
- Detects orphaned trades and creates accounts with correct IDs
- Shows loading skeleton while checking remote data
- Only allows onboarding for truly new users

This prevents the cycle where:
1. User opens app on new device
2. Gets sent to onboarding (localStorage empty)
3. Creates new account with different ID
4. New account syncs, orphaning existing trades
5. User loses access to their data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Chase silent sync failures — resilient removed/modified handling

Chase frequently re-IDs transactions, generating large `removed` lists.
The Plaid SDK returns `RemovedTransaction` objects that don't support
dict-style `['transaction_id']` access, which was crashing the entire
sync and preventing `last_synced_at` from updating.

Changes:
- plaid_integration.py: Safe extraction of removed IDs — tries dict
  access first, falls back to getattr(), logs any that can't be parsed
- main.py (modified loop): Per-row try/except so one bad record can't
  abort the entire sync; counts and logs failed rows separately
- main.py (removed loop): Same per-row try/except pattern with
  `removed_errors` counter and per-failure log line

All three changes together ensure a single malformed Plaid record
cannot silently kill a full sync cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(WL-086): Ryder Cup ABSENT day crash — Coltart/Valderrama

Root cause: startGame always set G.day=0; ABSENT matchPlayDays have no
opponentHoleScores, so endHole threw TypeError on first roll. Situation
text was set (garbled) but engine crashed silently before shot buttons
appeared, making it look like a hang on "Loading...".

Fixes:
- MatchPlayService.firstActiveDay(player): first non-ABSENT day index
- MatchPlayService.isDayAbsent(player, day): ABSENT format check
- buildContext: returns null for ABSENT days (no "against undefined")
- startGame: G.day = firstActiveDay(player), not hardcoded 0
- endHole: isDayAbsent guard before opponentHoleScores access
- Valderrama Day 3 parallelMatches: Coltart vs Woods added (was missing)

9 Gherkin scenarios: firstActiveDay and isDayAbsent edge cases green.

commit.message:
feat(analysis): SPRINT-PHASE2-CLV-EDGE-VALIDATION — CLV edge validation module (#128)

* docs(status): Ingestion → VERIFIED, DRIFT-M3 closed (Optimal API not in use)

* feat(analysis): SPRINT-PHASE2-CLV-EDGE-VALIDATION — CLV edge validation module

Implements three new modules under apps/api/src/analysis/edge-validation/:
- clvAnalyzer: computes CLV (p_final - p_market_devig) per pick, aggregates by market type
- edgeValidator: one-sample t-test (H₀: mean CLV = 0) with normal approximation
- edgeCalibrator: confidence intervals (CI = mean ± z·stdErr) for CLV estimates

46 new Vitest tests (613 total, 567 baseline). All fail-closed. 0 TS errors.

Sprint: SPRINT-PHASE2-CLV-EDGE-VALIDATION
Issue: UNI-16
